data.title: AvalonBay, Equity Residential weigh megamerger - report
data.description:
AvalonBay Communities (AVB) and Equity Residential (EQR) have held exploratory talks about merging two of the biggest apartment developers in the U.S., according to a media report on Thursday.

AvalonBay (AVB) stock rose 2.0%, and Equity Residential (EQR) stock gained 1.0% in premarket trading.

The deliberations are preliminary, and there's no guarantee they will result in a transaction, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The combination of the two apartment REITs could dramatically reshape the sector. Each of the companies has a market cap of ~$25B, making them the biggest apartment REITs in the U.S. in terms of market value.

As of March 31, 2026, AvalonBay (AVB) held direct or indirect ownership of ~98.3K apartments, while Equity Residential (EQR) owned ~85.2K apartment homes, the companies disclosed in their financial statements.

data.title: CNX Resources beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• CNX Resources press release (CNX): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.18 beats by $1.21.
  • Revenue of $786.65M (+854.8% Y/Y) beats by $223.56M.
  • In the first quarter of 2026, CNX generated $139 million in free cash flow  marking our 25 consecutive quarter of FCF generation.
  • Outlook :
  • We continue to expect total 2026 annual production volumes to be between 605 and 620 Bcfe and to turn-in-line 34 wells consisting of 27 Marcellus wells and 7 deep Utica wells.
  • Total 2026 base program capital expenditures are reaffirmed to be between $540 million and $570 million. Additionally for 2026, total capital expenditures includes the first of three annual payments of $16 million associated with the acquisition of the Utica rights beneath the legacy Apex Energy footprint that was entered into in Q3 2025.
  • Assuming April 15 NYMEX pricing of $3.64 per MMBtu, we expect to generate full year FCF of approximately $525 million, or $3.41 per share using our current shares outstanding plus the additional 12.4 million net shares expected to be issued to settle the remaining convertible notes. The modest decline in expected 2026 FCF is due to NYMEX pricing declining by $0.43 per MMBtu since our last update in January, offset in part by a $15 million increase in expected asset sales for the year.

data.title: Gildan Activewear Non-GAAP EPS of $0.43 beats by $0.08, revenue of $1.17B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Gildan Activewear press release (GIL): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.43 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $1.17B (+64.3% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • The year over year increase reflects the HanesBrands acquisition partially offset by integration initiatives (detailed thereafter) undertaken to optimize our manufacturing footprint and accelerate synergy capture.
  • Guidance
  • Revenue of $6.0 billion to $6.2 billion vs $6.14B consensus
  • Full year adjusted operating margin1 of approximately 20%;
  • Capex to come in at approximately 3% of net sales;
  • Adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $4.20 to $4.40 vs $4.31 consensus, an increase of approximately 20% to 25% year over year;
  • Free cash flow1 to be above $850 million.
  •

data.title: Molson Coors beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Molson Coors press release (TAP): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.62 beats by $0.26.
  • Revenue of $2.35B (+2.2% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Financial volume decreased 2.9%, due to lower shipments in both the Americas and EMEA&APAC segments.
  • Brand volumes decreased 3.1%, including a 3.0% decrease in the Americas segment as well as a 3.4% decrease in the EMEA&APAC segment.

  • 2026 OUTLOOK

    We continue to expect to achieve the following targets for full year 2026 despite the inherent uncertainties that exist with inflationary commodity cost pressures and uncertainty in the global macroeconomic environment.

    • Net sales: flat, plus or minus 1% versus 2025 on a constant currency basis vs. estimated growth of -0.15% Y/Y.
    • Underlying income (loss) before income taxes: decline in the range of 15% to 18% versus 2025 on a constant currency basis.
    • Underlying earnings per share: decline in the range of 11% to 15% versus 2025 vs. estimated growth of  -13.14% Y/Y.
    • Capital expenditures: $650 million incurred, plus or minus 5%.
    • Underlying free cash flow: $1.1 billion, plus or minus 10%.
    • Underlying depreciation and amortization: $720 million, plus or minus 5%.
    • Consolidated net interest expense: $260 million, plus or minus 5%.
    • Underlying effective tax rate: in the range of 22% to 24% for 2026.

    The Company's outlook includes the following considerations:

    • In the second quarter, U.S. financial volumes are expected to be between 6% and 9% lower than 2025, trailing anticipated brand volume trends. Financial volumes are expected to outpace brand volumes in the second half of the year.
    • In COGS, the cost of Midwest Premium is expected to be inflationary in each quarter of the year, with the largest increase currently expected in the second quarter.
    • MG&A expenses are expected to increase compared to 2025 during the second through fourth quarters, driven by higher incentive compensation expenses with the most significant increase expected in the second quarter.

data.title: Caterpillar Non-GAAP EPS of $5.54 beats by $0.90, revenue of $17.4B beats by $980M
data.description:
• Caterpillar press release (CAT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $5.54 beats by $0.90.
  • Revenue of $17.4B (+22.5% Y/Y) beats by $980M.
  • First-quarter 2026 profit per share of $5.47
  • Operating profit margin was 17.7% for the first quarter of 2026, compared with 18.1% for the first quarter of 2025. Adjusted operating profit margin was 18.0% for the first quarter of 2026, compared with 18.3% for the first quarter of 2025.  
  • Deployed $5.7 billion of cash for share repurchases and dividends in the first quarter

data.title: CNH Industrial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.01, revenue of $3.83B misses by $30M
data.description:
• CNH Industrial press release (CNH): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $3.83B (flat Y/Y) misses by $30M.

  • Consequently, the Company is reaffirming its 2026 outlook:

    • Agriculture segment net sales down 5% to flat year-over-year, including +2% currency translation effects
    • Agriculture segment adjusted EBIT margin between 4.5% and 5.5%
    • Construction segment net sales about flat year-over-year, including +1% currency translation effects
    • Construction segment adjusted EBIT margin between 1.0% and 2.0%
    • Free Cash Flow of Industrial Activities between $150 million and $350 million
    • Adjusted diluted EPS between $0.35 to $0.45 vs $0.42 consensus

data.title: Cigna posts Q1 beat as Evernorth powers topline growth
data.description:
The Cigna Group (CI) reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 financials and increased its full-year earnings outlook on Thursday, while its Evernorth Health Services unit, which houses its pharmacy benefit manager Express Scripts, drove the topline growth.

The Bloomfield, Connecticut-based health insurer generated $68.5B in revenue for the quarter with ~5% YoY growth, beating the consensus by $2.32B as the Evernorth segment added $58.4B, exceeding $56.7B projected by analysts, according to Bloomberg data.

Meanwhile, its insurance unit, Cigna Healthcare, recorded $11.5B in revenue, beating the consensus of $11.4B despite a ~21% YoY decline attributed to the sale of the company’s Medicare products to Health Care Services Corporation in March 2025.

Cigna Healthcare disclosed a medical care ratio of 79.8% for the period compared to 81% in the consensus and down from 82.2% in the prior year quarter, primarily due to the HCSC transaction.

The company reported $7.79 of adjusted EPS for the period, up ~16% from the year-ago quarter and exceeding the Street forecasts by $0.18, driven by Cigna Healthcare, where pre-tax margin rose to 13.2% from 8.9% in Q1 2025, thanks to its U.S. Healthcare business.

Looking ahead, Cigna (CI) projects at least $30.35 per share of adjusted income from operations, up from $30.25 in the prior forecast and slightly better than $30.33 in the consensus.

data.title: Veradermics prices upsized $384M public offering and $30M private placement at $100/share
data.description:
Veradermics (MANE) has announced a public offering of 3.84M shares of its common stock priced at $100.00 per share and will receive approximately $384.4 million in gross proceeds from this offering, which is expected to close on May 1, 2026.

Veradermics has also given underwriters a 30-day option to buy an additional 576,568 shares at the same public offering price, minus underwriting fees.Additionally, Veradermics has entered a securities purchase agreement to sell 0.3M pre-funded warrants at $99.99999 each, representing a minor discount from the public offering price. 

This private placement aims at certain entities affiliated with Suvretta Capital and is projected to yield approximately $30.0 million before deducting fees. 

The private placement is also scheduled to close on May 1, 2026.
